engineGuide.h2: Peugeot 5008 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Peugeot 5008 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The DW10FC BlueHDi replaced the older DW10 in Gen 2, bringing SCR/AdBlue tech and Euro 6 compliance - now the most searched diesel replacement for GT trims.
compatibleVariants: GT, GT Line, GT Premium, Allure Premium
commonFailures: SCR calibration faults triggering limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10FC Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The robust DW10 powered Gen 1 5008 diesels, known for strong torque and towing capability - still a high-volume replacement request from early-model owners.
commonFailures: Injector seal leaks causing rough running
EGR cooler clogging at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Diesel
entries.history: The DV5 BlueHDi arrived with Gen 2 as the new entry-level diesel, offering improved economy and lower emissions for family SUV buyers.
commonFailures: EGR valve clogging on stop-start driving
DPF regeneration issues on urban use
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV5 Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: The DV6 HDi was the workhorse diesel for Gen 1 5008s, widely used across the range - now a common replacement due to high-mileage family use.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration failure on short trips
entries.cta: Get quotes for DV6 Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The EP6FDT PureTech brought direct injection and turbocharging to Gen 2 petrol 5008s - popular but requires timing chain attention for longevity.

entries.title: pressure fuel pump failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6FDT Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The EP6DT THP powered Gen 1 performance petrol 5008s - an interference engine where timing chain failure can cause catastrophic valve damage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6DT Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The EB2ADTS PureTech is the entry-level petrol for Gen 2 5008s, using a wet timing belt design that requires strict maintenance intervals.
commonFailures: Timing belt degradation in oil (112k miles/10yrs)
Belt fragments causing oil pump blockage
Premature wear if service intervals missed
entries.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADTS Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The naturally aspirated EW10A was the base petrol for early Gen 1 5008s - simple, reliable, but now less common in replacement searches.
commonFailures: Water pump leaks at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for EW10A Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Plug-in Hybrid
entries.history: The EP6FDT PHEV powers the Gen 2 plug-in hybrid 5008, combining petrol and electric drive for low-emission family motoring with 225hp combined output.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EP6FDT PHEV Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel-Electric Hybrid
entries.history: The HYbrid4 system paired a DW10 diesel with a rear electric motor for Gen 1's pioneering four-wheel-drive hybrid 5008 RXH - now a specialist replacement.
commonFailures: Battery pack capacity loss over time
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW10 HYbrid4 Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Peugeot 5008 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Peugeot 5008's engine reliability varies significantly by generation and fuel type. The 1.6 THP (EP6DT) timing chain, 1.2 PureTech (EB2ADTS) wet belt, and BlueHDi (DW10FC/DV5) AdBlue systems dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a maximum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Peugeot 5008 1.6 THP Timing Chain Failure - Why EP6DT Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 5008 Gen 1 GT Line, Sport, Premium (2009-2017, EP6DT)
problems.rootCause: The rear-mounted timing chain tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that can jump teeth and cause catastrophic interference engine damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; prevents engine destruction

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if valve damage has occurred; restores full reliability

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling from the rear of the engine on cold starts, act immediately. On a Gen 1 5008 worth £3,000-£6,000, a preventative chain kit from a specialist is the smart investment. If the chain has already failed, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components is more cost-effective than dealer repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6DT Peugeot 5008 engine prices

problems.h4: Peugeot 5008 1.2 PureTech Timing Belt Failure - EB2ADTS Wet Belt Risk
problems.rootCause: The timing belt runs in oil and can degrade prematurely, shedding fragments that block the oil pump pickup, causing oil starvation and engine seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Belt & Oil Pump Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wet belt, oil pump, flush system before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Essential maintenance; prevents catastrophic engine damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated belt spec, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if oil starvation has caused bearing damage

problems.recommendation: Strictly adhere to the 112,000-mile/10-year belt change interval - or earlier if you do short journeys. On a Gen 2 5008, a specialist belt service is far cheaper than engine replacement. If the engine has seized, a rebuilt unit with the latest belt specification is the reliable solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EB2ADTS Peugeot 5008 engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 5008 BlueHDi AdBlue System Fault - DW10FC/DV5 SCR Issues
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 5008 Gen 2 Allure, GT, GT Premium (2017-2024, DW10FC/DV5)
problems.rootCause: AdBlue injectors crystallise or fail, triggering SCR system faults that force the engine into limp mode and eventually prevent restarts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace injector, clean SCR system, recalibrate ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before engine management lockdown

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with AdBlue system check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if prolonged limp mode has caused secondary damage

problems.recommendation: Don't ignore AdBlue warning lights. On a family 5008 used for school runs, a specialist SCR service can resolve the issue before it strands you. If the engine has suffered from extended limp-mode operation, a rebuilt engine with full emissions calibration is the dependable fix.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt DW10FC Peugeot 5008 diesel engine prices

problems.h4: Peugeot 5008 DPF Regeneration Failure - HDi/BlueHDi Particulate Filter Issues
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 5008 Gen 1 & Gen 2 diesel variants (2009-2024, DV6/DW10/DV5)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents active DPF regeneration, causing soot buildup that eventually blocks the filter and triggers power loss or limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic-led regeneration cycle or off-car cleaning
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if blockage is moderate; prevents replacement

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit new or cleaned DPF with ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine is otherwise healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with DPF system check, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if DPF failure has caused turbo or EGR damage

problems.recommendation: If your 5008 is used mainly for short trips, schedule regular motorway runs to aid DPF regeneration. A specialist cleaning is cost-effective for early blockages. If the DPF failure has led to turbo or EGR damage, a rebuilt engine with full emissions system verification is the reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 5008 diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Peugeot 5008 High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - EP6FDT/EP6DT HPFP Issues
problems.affectedModels: Peugeot 5008 Gen 1 & Gen 2 petrol turbo variants (2009-2024, EP6DT/EP6FDT)
problems.rootCause: The high-pressure fuel pump wears internally, causing low fuel pressure, misfires, rough running, and eventually engine stalling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, recalibrate fuel system, test pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before secondary damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with tested fuel system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if low fuel pressure has caused piston or valve damage

problems.recommendation: If you experience misfires or power loss under load on a turbo petrol 5008, get the fuel pressure tested immediately. A specialist HPFP replacement can restore performance. If the engine has suffered from prolonged lean running, a rebuilt unit with full fuel system verification is the dependable fix.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EP6FDT Peugeot 5008 petrol engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Peugeot 5008
finalCta.paragraph: The Peugeot 5008's engine reliability depends heavily on maintenance history and driving patterns. Whether you're facing a timing chain rattle, AdBlue warning, or DPF light, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Peugeot 5008 specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a maximum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Peugeot 5008 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Peugeot 5008 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a maximum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Peugeot 5008 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement fitted to the Peugeot 5008 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Peugeot 5008 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L BlueHDi (DV5) arrived with the Gen 2 5008 as the new entry-level diesel, offering improved fuel economy and Euro 6 emissions compliance for family SUV buyers seeking lower running costs.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve clogging on stop-start driving; AdBlue system warnings at 50k+ miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 5008 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 5008 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L HDi/BlueHDi (DV6/EP6FDT diesel) powered both generations of the 5008, serving as the versatile mid-range diesel option with strong torque for towing and family hauling.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration failure on short journeys; turbo actuator sticking at higher mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 5008 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 5008 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L HDi/BlueHDi (DW10/DW10FC) is the backbone of the 5008 diesel range, delivering robust performance and towing capability across both generations with advanced SCR/AdBlue tech on Gen 2.
compatibleModels: Gen 1 Allure/Sport/Premium/RXH (2009-2017); Gen 2 GT/GT Line/GT Premium
commonFailurePoints: AdBlue injector failure on DW10FC; injector seal leaks on DW10; DPF saturation on urban use
items.cta: Get quotes for Peugeot 5008 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Peugeot 5008 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech (EB2ADTS) is the entry-level petrol for Gen 2 5008s, using a turbocharged three-cylinder design with a wet timing belt that requires strict adherence to service intervals.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt degradation in oil (112k miles/10yrs); belt fragments blocking oil pump pickup
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 5008 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 5008 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L THP/PureTech (EP6DT/EP6FDT) powered performance-oriented petrol 5008s across both generations, offering strong power but requiring attention to timing chain (Gen 1) or tensioner (Gen 2) maintenance.
compatibleModels: Gen 1 GT Line/Sport/Premium (2009-2017); Gen 2 GT Line/GT
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear on EP6DT; high-pressure fuel pump failure; turbo oil feed leaks
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 5008 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 5008 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 2.0L petrol (EW10A) was the base petrol engine for early Gen 1 5008s, offering simple, reliable performance before turbocharged units became standard.
commonFailurePoints: Water pump leaks at higher mileage; coil pack failure; generally robust with proper maintenance
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 5008 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Peugeot 5008 1.6L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The plug-in hybrid 1.6L PureTech (EP6FDT PHEV) powers Gen 2 PHEV variants, combining petrol and electric drive for low-emission family motoring with 225hp combined output and electric-only capability.

families: DW10FC - 2.0L BlueHDi (GT/GT Premium), SCR/AdBlue calibration critical

DV6 - 1.6L HDi (Gen 1), budget diesel with DPF regeneration needs
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and towing capability

High UK replacement demand on Allure and GT diesel variants

EB2ADTS - 1.2L PureTech turbo (Gen 2), wet belt requires strict servicing

EW10A - 2.0L NA petrol (Gen 1), simple and reliable but less common now

EB2ADTS wet belt degradation on Gen 2 (preventative service critical)

families: EP6FDT PHEV - 1.6L plug-in hybrid (PHEV GT/Premium), battery health check essential

HYbrid4: Diesel engine front, electric motor rear, parallel hybrid operation

engineYears.h2: Peugeot 5008 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Peugeot 5008's production timeline (2009-2024), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original 5008 launched with proven HDi diesels and the turbocharged THP petrol, establishing the model's family SUV credentials.
keyChanges: Peugeot 5008 first generation launched on PF2 platform
Initial engines: 1.6 HDi (DV6), 2.0 HDi (DW10), 1.6 THP (EP6DT), 2.0 petrol (EW10A)
knownFor: Robust DW10 diesel with strong towing capability
EP6DT THP timing chain tensioner issues emerging at higher mileage
Simple EW10A petrol reliable but underpowered for 7-seater use
High mileage examples now entering replacement phase
years.cta: Get quotes for 2009-2013 Peugeot 5008 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 5008 1.6 HDi engine replacement • Peugeot 5008 2.0 HDi rebuild cost • Peugeot 5008 THP timing chain fix

years.description: The 2011 facelift brought styling updates and the pioneering HYbrid4 diesel-electric system on the RXH variant.
keyChanges: Mid-cycle facelift with updated front/rear styling
Introduction of HYbrid4 diesel-electric AWD system on RXH
e-HDi micro-hybrid technology added to select diesel variants
knownFor: HYbrid4 RXH a niche but innovative four-wheel-drive hybrid SUV
e-HDi stop-start systems requiring battery and alternator attention
DW10 injector seal leaks becoming common on high-mileage examples
EP6DT timing chain failures driving replacement enquiries
years.ticker: Peugeot 5008 HYbrid4 engine repair • Peugeot 5008 e-HDi battery replacement • Peugeot 5008 THP engine rebuild

years.description: The all-new Gen 2 5008 on EMP2 platform introduced downsized PureTech petrol and advanced BlueHDi diesel engines with Euro 6 compliance.
keyChanges: Complete redesign on EMP2 platform with modern styling
New engine range: 1.2 PureTech (EB2ADTS), 1.6 BlueHDi (EP6FDT diesel), 2.0 BlueHDi (DW10FC)
Introduction of EAT8 automatic transmission across range
knownFor: EB2ADTS wet timing belt requiring strict 112k-mile/10-year service
EP6FDT diesel timing chain tensioner attention needed at higher mileage
DW10FC AdBlue system faults emerging on early Gen 2 examples
Strong demand for rebuilt engines on high-mileage family SUVs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Peugeot 5008 replacement engines
years.ticker: Peugeot 5008 PureTech belt replacement • Peugeot 5008 BlueHDi AdBlue fix • Peugeot 5008 2.0 diesel rebuild

years.description: Plug-in hybrid variants arrived alongside minor updates, expanding the 5008's electrified options while refining existing BlueHDi and PureTech engines.
keyChanges: Introduction of PHEV GT and GT Premium with EP6FDT PHEV powertrain
Minor facelift updates to styling and infotainment (2023)
Continued refinement of BlueHDi emissions systems for Euro 6d compliance
mainEngines: EP6FDT - 1.6L BlueHDi / PHEV (GT Line, PHEV GT)
EP6FDT PHEV - 1.6L plug-in hybrid (PHEV GT, GT Premium)
knownFor: PHEV battery health checks essential during engine replacement
DW10FC AdBlue injector failures on high-mileage GT variants
EB2ADTS belt degradation cases rising as first Gen 2 engines age
Growing replacement demand on PHEV and high-spec diesel variants
years.ticker: Peugeot 5008 PHEV battery check • Peugeot 5008 BlueHDi SCR calibration • Peugeot 5008 PureTech engine rebuild

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Peugeot 5008 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a maximum 12‑month warranty from UK Peugeot 5008 specialists.

faq.h2: Peugeot 5008 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Peugeot 5008 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Peugeot 5008 engine replacement costs range from £495-£1,050 for used units to £1,500-£3,100 for rebuilt engines, depending on engine code, variant and supplier.
keyPoints: Used engines: £495-£1,050 supply only
Rebuilt engines: £1,500-£3,100 typical range
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour
PHEV/hybrid units cost significantly more
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 5008 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: What engine codes fit my Peugeot 5008?
items.answer: Common Peugeot 5008 engine codes include EP6FDT, DW10FC, DV5, EB2ADTS, EP6DT and DW10 - enter your registration for instant compatibility verification.
keyPoints: Gen 1: DV6, DW10, EP6DT, EW10A
Gen 2: EB2ADTS, EP6FDT, DV5, DW10FC
PHEV: EP6FDT PHEV; Hybrid4: DW10 HYbrid4
DVLA lookup ensures exact code match
items.cta: Verify your Peugeot 5008 engine code instantly

items.question: How long does Peugeot 5008 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most Peugeot 5008 engines dispatch within 1-3 working days, with supply & fit appointments typically completed within 2-4 working days nationwide.
keyPoints: Used engines often available for next-day dispatch
Rebuilt units: 1-3 day lead time typical
Supply & fit booking: 2-4 days depending on location
PHEV/hybrid may require longer for specialist calibration
items.cta: Book your Peugeot 5008 engine fitting appointment

items.question: What warranty comes with Peugeot 5008 replacement engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt Peugeot 5008 engines typically include a maximum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; used engines often carry shorter 3-6 month cover.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: 12-month unlimited mileage standard
Reconditioned: 3-12 months depending on supplier
Used: Typically 3-6 months, verify terms
Warranty valid UK-wide with approved fitting
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 5008 engine warranty terms

items.question: Can I upgrade my Peugeot 5008 engine to a more powerful one?
items.answer: Engine upgrades on the Peugeot 5008 are possible but require ECU recalibration and compatibility checks - consult a specialist before proceeding.
keyPoints: DW10FC can replace DV5 with ECU adaptation
PHEV conversions not feasible post-factory
Performance tunes require supporting modifications
Always verify emissions compliance for MOT
items.cta: Enquire about Peugeot 5008 engine upgrade options

items.question: How do I know if my Peugeot 5008 needs a new engine or just repair?
items.answer: Get a full diagnostic - compression tests, oil analysis and fault code reading determine whether repair or Peugeot 5008 engine replacement is the cost-effective solution.
keyPoints: Compression below 12:1 suggests major wear
Metal in oil indicates bearing failure
Timing chain/belt failure usually requires replacement
Specialist diagnostics prevent unnecessary rebuilds
items.cta: Get a free Peugeot 5008 engine assessment

items.question: Are Peugeot 5008 replacement engines MOT compliant?
items.answer: All Peugeot 5008 replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket include emissions certification to ensure full MOT compliance for UK road use.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines tested to original emissions specs
BlueHDi/SCR systems calibrated post-installation
Documentation provided for MOT tester verification
Non-compliant units risk MOT failure and fines
items.cta: Ensure your Peugeot 5008 engine is MOT-ready

items.question: Do you supply Peugeot 5008 engines with gearbox?
items.answer: Peugeot 5008 engine and gearbox packages are available from select suppliers, typically offering 10-15% savings versus separate purchases.
keyPoints: EAT6/EAT8 auto compatibility verified pre-dispatch
Manual gearbox options available for Gen 1/Gen 2
Package deals include clutch/flywheel where applicable
Confirm fitment details with chosen supplier
items.cta: Compare Peugeot 5008 engine and gearbox deals

items.question: Can I fit a Peugeot 5008 engine myself?
items.answer: Supply-only Peugeot 5008 engines are available for DIY fitment, but professional installation is recommended to maintain warranty validity and ensure correct calibration.

Professional fit required for PHEV/hybrid systems
ECU coding often needed post-installation
Warranty may be voided by non-approved fitting

items.answer: Contact EngineMarket specialists for custom Peugeot 5008 engine sourcing - rare codes and legacy units can often be located within the UK network.
